name: Crown molding
description: A decorative horizontal trim run along the top perimeter of an interior wall, in the single course directly below the ceiling, where wall meets ceiling. Crown molding is a classical refinement detail — it softens the otherwise sharp 90-degree intersection between the vertical wall plane and the horizontal ceiling plane and adds a finer level of scale to a room that would otherwise read as a bare box. Profile is stepped or curved: an inverted oak stair facing into the room reads as a coved cornice, while a spruce slab tucked flush against the ceiling reads as a flat picture rail. The trim wraps every interior wall continuously, breaking only at door and window heads. Found in renaissance halls, Victorian parlours, and Gothic chapter rooms.
